data.title: As a practitioner, I can trust the clarity gate produces correct pass/fail decisions
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Validate and calibrate the clarity gate by running the heuristic against closed tickets and dogfooding sprint on well-defined stories.
**Why**: The heuristic threshold must be verified empirically before practitioners rely on it. Without calibration, false positives/negatives undermine trust in the gate.
**Scope**:
- IN: Run ticket-clarity-check.sh against 20 recent closed tickets, verify distribution, dogfood sprint on 3 stories
- OUT: Ongoing tuning post-release (handled by practitioners adjusting the config threshold)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ticket-clarity-check.sh run against 20 recent closed tickets with descriptions >=200 chars shows at least 15 scoring >=5
  ← Satisfies: "run ticket-clarity-check.sh against 20 recent closed tickets... confirm at least 15 score >=5"
- When this story is complete, no ticket with description <100 chars scores >=5 when run through ticket-clarity-check.sh
  ← Satisfies: "confirm no ticket with description <100 chars scores >=5"
- When this story is complete, /dso:sprint has been run on 3 well-defined stories (not epics) and completed the full pipeline: clarity gate → complexity eval → batch execution → verification → commit
  ← Satisfies: "a practitioner can run /dso:sprint <story-id> on a well-defined story and observe... the same pipeline"

## Considerations

- None
